sources.name: TASVideos Game Resources — NES / Super Mario Bros. 2
sources.notes: Primary source. Documents walk (24 sub-px/frame = 1.5 px/f), run (36 = 2.25 px/f), Toad with item (43 = 2.69 px/f). Sub-pixel system is 16ths.

notes: SMB2 is based on Doki Doki Panic (Famicom, 1987), a completely different game. NOT a sequel to SMB1 mechanics.
The fundamental difference from SMB1/3: NO STOMPING. Combat is throw-based — pick up enemies/items, throw them.
Four distinct playable characters with different stats. Arguably the first mainstream platformer with this design.
Jump and gravity values are documented qualitatively ('Luigi jumps highest', 'Toad runs fastest') but exact ROM values need verification.
Sub-pixel system: 16 sub-pixels per pixel (same as SMB1/SMB3). Confirmed by TASVideos.

subpixel_system.source: TASVideos SMB2 Game Resources: '16 subpixels = 1 pixel'

walk_speed.notes: Base walk speed. Identical to SMB1 walk speed.

run_speed.notes: Run speed when holding B. Slightly lower than SMB1 run (2.5 px/f) but higher than SMB1 walk.

toad_item_carry_speed.notes: Toad's unique trait: runs faster while carrying heavy items (enemy, mushroom block, key, etc.) than normal. This counter-intuitive mechanic makes Toad a strong speedrun character.

characters._note: Four playable characters with meaningfully different physics. This is the key design innovation of SMB2.

mario.description: Balanced. The 'default' character. Medium jump height, medium speed, medium throw distance.

luigi.description: Highest jump of the four. Slippery — higher inertia, slides more on landing. Runs slower.
luigi.inertia: high — slides noticeably on direction changes and landings
luigi.notes: Luigi's high jump + high inertia is a tradeoff: he can reach higher platforms but is harder to stop precisely. Experts use his float for extended platform traversal.

princess_peach.description: Can float briefly by holding jump at peak — unique ability. Slowest runner. Weakest throw.

float_mechanic.description: Holding jump at jump apex causes Peach to float horizontally for approximately 1.5 seconds (90 frames).
float_mechanic.notes: Float halts vertical descent while maintaining horizontal momentum. Allows crossing large horizontal gaps impossible for other characters.

princess_peach.notes: Peach's float makes her excellent for precision platforming and gap-crossing. Weakest in combat-heavy sections.

toad.description: Fastest runner. Weakest jump (lowest, least float). Fastest item pickup and throw. Speed bonus when carrying items.
toad.jump_type: fast rise, fast fall — almost no float

item_carry_bonus.notes: Toad's defining stat. Carrying 'heavy' items (enemies, blocks, keys) gives a speed boost over normal running.

jump.notes: Jump height varies by character and by how long jump is held. All characters have variable height jump.

approximate_heights_px.notes: Heights approximate from gameplay observation. VERIFY against ROM.

gravity.notes: Gravity constant not documented in reviewed sources. VERIFY against Data Crystal RAM map.
gravity.qualitative: Falls feel similar to SMB1 — relatively fast fall. Peach's float mechanic directly counteracts gravity at jump peak.

combat_system._note: NO STOMPING in SMB2. This is the defining design departure from SMB1/SMB3.
combat_system.primary_attack: Pick up enemy or item, throw it at another enemy

pickup_mechanic.description: Hold B to 'pull' a vegetable from the ground or grab an enemy from behind. Release B to throw.
pickup_mechanic.notes: Different enemies have different throw behavior — some explode, some bounce, some can be ridden.

exceptions: POW Block: throw at the ground to damage all enemies on screen
Starman: brief invincibility with contact damage (same as SMB1)
Birdo eggs: Birdo fires eggs that can be caught and thrown back

notable_enemies.phanto: Invincible key-guardian — chases player relentlessly when holding the key. Drop the key and Phanto stops.
notable_enemies.shy_guy: Can be picked up and thrown. Different colors have different resistances.
notable_enemies.birdo: Boss that fires eggs. Must throw eggs back at her.

item_pull_frames_approx.notes: Toad picks up items faster. Approximate values from gameplay observation. VERIFY.

vegetables.notes: Vegetables grow from the ground in specific spots. Pulling one takes a few frames. Throw speed and damage vary by vegetable type.

notes: 7 worlds, each with multiple stages. Some stages have above-ground and underground sections.
Doors connect areas within a stage (using Potions to create doors).
Boss at end of each world — all named 'Birdo' (sub-bosses) and Wart (final boss).
POW blocks and certain items are limited within each stage — resource scarcity creates planning.

_verification_checklist: Exact jump velocities for all 4 characters (ROM values)
Exact pickup frame counts per character
design_notes_for_giants_drink: The four-character physics differentiation is the core design insight of SMB2. Each character isn't just 'faster' or 'slower' — they represent genuinely different strategies. Toad is for speed routing; Luigi is for gap navigation; Peach is for precision platforming; Mario is the safe default.

The throw-based combat vs SMB1 stomp combat is a complete combat paradigm shift with the same basic movement system. The same physics engine supports two very different games depending on how enemies respond to contact.
Toad's item-carry speed bonus is counter-intuitive (carrying = faster) but creates an interesting mechanical consequence: strategic players pick up items NOT to use them but to use the speed boost.
Peach's float creates a platform-design vocabulary unavailable in other Mario games: 'float gap' — a horizontal distance that only Peach can clear, creating character-specific routing.
The Phanto key-guardian mechanic is a masterclass in tension design: the reward (key) is immediately stressful to use because carrying it triggers pursuit. This is pure game design elegance.
